motivation:
The POST request application method caught my attention—it's a perfect filter for technical initiative. I want to join XBorg because building for passionate communities is what drives me. At YoYoRaven, I built an international brand serving 50+ countries in a niche community, which gave me a deep understanding of what makes groups engage over time. My Flow Theory Visuals platform builds on this: I created a video-sharing and monetization system for social dance communities across Asia-Pacific, covering 20+ festivals. I built it primarily for my own needs as a creator, which meant I was also the end user—feeling the friction, iterating quickly, and prioritizing what actually mattered in practice. What excites me about XBorg is applying this same builder-as-user mindset to a new niche. While my background isn’t rooted in esports specifically, I’ve spent years building and participating in highly engaged niche communities where identity, loyalty, and shared culture matter deeply. The mechanics that make dance, yoyo, or creative communities thrive are the same ones that power esports fandom.

I have spent several years building enterprise SaaS at Vincit, delivering features end-to-end from design to production. While NestJS was new to me, I completed this challenge specifically to demonstrate rapid framework adoption—my experience with ABP Framework (Entity Framework, Angular) and Express.js meant the underlying patterns transferred naturally. I thrive in environments where product thinking matters as much as code quality. React/Next.js and Node.js are part of my daily toolkit, and I have production experience with CI/CD pipelines and event-driven systems using RabbitMQ. I ship working code and believe that perfect is the enemy of shipped.
